parts.title: Part 1: Questions 1–10
parts.instructions: Complete the notes below. Write ONE WORD AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer.

parts.text: Good for people who are especially keen on
is a good place for a drink

rows.content: In Bow Street next to the cinema

parts.text:
Staff are very friendly

Need to pay £50 deposit

A limited selection of

questions.question: Good for people who are especially keen on ___
The ___ is a good place for a drink
___ food, good for sharing
A limited selection of ___ food on the menu
The ___ (restaurant name)
At the top of a ___
All the ___ are very good
Only uses ___ ingredients
Set lunch costs £___ per person
Portions of probably ___ size

parts.title: Part 2: Questions 11–20
parts.instructions: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

questions.question: 11. Heather says pottery differs from other art forms because
options: A. It lasts longer in the ground.
B. It is practised by more people.
C. It can be repaired more easily.
questions.answer: A. It lasts longer in the ground.

questions.question: 12. Archaeologists sometimes identify the use of ancient pottery from
options: A. The clay it was made with.
B. The marks that are on it.
C. The basic shape of it.
questions.answer: B. The marks that are on it.

questions.question: 13. Some people join Heather’s pottery class because they want to
options: A. Create an item that looks very old.
B. Find something that they are good at.
C. Make something that will outlive them.
questions.answer: C. Make something that will outlive them.

questions.question: 14. What does Heather value most about being a potter?
options: A. Its calming effect
B. Its messy nature
C. Its physical benefits

questions.question: 15. Most of the visitors to Edelman Pottery
options: A. Bring friends to join courses.
B. Have never made a pot before.
C. Try to learn techniques too quickly.
questions.answer: B. Have never made a pot before.

questions.question: 16. Heather reminds her visitors that they should
options: A. Put on their aprons.
B. Change their clothes.
C. Take off their jewellery.

questions.question: 17–18. Which TWO things does Heather explain about kilns?
options: A. What their function is
B. When they were invented
C. Ways of keeping them safe
D. Where to put one in your home
E. What some people use instead of one
answer: E. What some people use instead of one

questions.question: 19–20. Which points does Heather make about a potter’s tools?
options: A. Some are hard to hold.
B. Some are worth buying.
C. Some are essential items.
D. Some have memorable names.
E. Some are available for use by participants.
answer: E. Some are available for use by participants.

parts.title: Part 3: Questions 21–30
parts.instructions: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

questions.question: 21–22. Which TWO things do the students both believe are responsible for the increase in loneliness?
options: A. Social media
B. Smaller nuclear families
C. Urban design
D. Longer lifespans
E. A mobile workforce

questions.question: 23–24. Which TWO health risks associated with loneliness do the students agree are based on solid evidence?
options: A. A weakened immune system
B. Dementia
C. Cancer
D. Obesity
E. Cardiovascular disease

questions.question: 25–26. Which TWO opinions do both the students express about the evolutionary theory of loneliness?
options: A. It has little practical relevance.
B. It needs further investigation.
C. It is misleading.
D. It should be more widely accepted.
E. It is difficult to understand.
answer: A. It has little practical relevance.

questions.question: 27. When comparing loneliness to depression, the students
options: A. Doubt that there will ever be a medical cure for loneliness.
B. Claim that the link between loneliness and mental health is overstated.
C. Express frustration that loneliness is not taken more seriously.
questions.answer: A. Doubt that there will ever be a medical cure for loneliness.

questions.question: 28. Why do the students decide to start their presentation with an example from their own experience?
options: A. To explain how difficult loneliness can be
B. To highlight a situation that most students will recognise
C. To emphasise that feeling lonely is more common for men than women
questions.answer: B. To highlight a situation that most students will recognise

questions.question: 29. The students agree that talking to strangers is a good strategy for dealing with loneliness because
options: A. It creates a sense of belonging.
B. It builds self-confidence.
C. It makes people feel more positive.
questions.answer: A. It creates a sense of belonging.

questions.question: 30. The students find it difficult to understand why solitude is considered to be
options: A. Similar to loneliness.
B. Necessary for mental health.
C. An enjoyable experience.

parts.title: Part 4: Questions 31–40
parts.instructions: Complete the notes below. Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.

questions.question: Pollution from ___ on the river bank (industrial context).
In 1957, the River Thames was declared biologically ___
Seals and even a ___ have been seen in the River Thames.
Riverside warehouses are converted to restaurants and ___
In Los Angeles, plans to build a riverside ___
Display ___ projects (Los Angeles).
In Paris, ___ are created on the sides of the river every summer.
Over 2 billion passengers already travel by ___ in cities around the world.
Goods can be moved by barges and electric ___ (instead of only road transport).
…or future ___ (transport).
